title: levante package walkthrough
text: Permission access data granted via Redivis, data-sharing platform used LEVANTE datasets. Public releases LEVANTE datasets Redivis include least four tables: participants, scores, surveys, trials. Table 1. Redivis tables commonly read data frames levante

what: Using the levante package to load LEVANTE data
title: levante package walkthrough
text: table Redivis can read R data frame using specific levante get_() function. get_() function takes least two arguments, including dataset name (required) version number (defaults current version unless otherwise specified). First, load levante packages, needed. , use get_participants() obtain child participant ids, approximate birthdates, associated caregiver teacher ids. get_participants() first levante function code. run , ’ll prompted authenticate pop-browser Redivis (academic database manager use LEVANTE) ensure right permissions access dataset. Subsequent levante calls dataset within session require additional authentication. Use get_scores() obtain scored cognitive task data.  Use get_trials() access trial-level data. Use get_surveys() access item-level survey data. Use get_parameters() access IRT item parameters used LEVANTE scoring. Finally, researchers accessing LEVANTE data can use get_raw_data() access additional tables within private datasets. get_raw_data() general function can pull table specify, provided table exists. pulling raw data, please note table names different processed data. Check dataset page ensure table exists.
code: library(levante) #> LEVANTE measures are covered under a CC-BY-NC-SA 4.0 license (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-sa/4.0/deed.en). Measures adapted from the Rapid Online Assessment of Reading (Language Sounds, Sentence Reading, Word Reading) are covered under a Stanford Academic License (https://github.com/yeatmanlab/roar-mp/blob/main/LICENSE). library(dplyr) #>  #> Attaching package: 'dplyr' #> The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': #>  #>     filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from 'package:base': #>  #>     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2) theme_set(theme_classic()) # Pull participant data from Redivis and save it to a data frame called "participants" participants <- get_participants(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current")  #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table participants  # Now let's do some preliminary checks on the participants data participants |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of participants from each dataset — the levante-data-example and private dataset data releases only contain data from a single dataset, but the public data releases contain multiple datasets stapled together #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main    15 # Pull scored data from Redivis and save it to a data frame called "scores" scores <- get_scores(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table scores  # Now let's do some preliminary checks on the scores data scores |> count(task_id, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of scores for each task. for a list of item_task abbreviations, see Table 1 here: https://researcher.levante-network.org/measures/direct-child-measures #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>    task_id                      n #>    <chr>                    <int> #>  1 vocab                        5 #>  2 hearts-and-flowers           4 #>  3 trog                         4 #>  4 egma-math                    3 #>  5 matrix-reasoning             3 #>  6 memory-game                  3 #>  7 mental-rotation              3 #>  8 same-different-selection     3 #>  9 theory-of-mind               3 #> 10 sre                          1 #> 11 swr                          1 # This plot returns scores by age fro each task. ggplot(scores, aes(x = age, y = score)) +   facet_wrap(vars(task_id)) +   geom_point() #> Warning: Removed 1 row containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_point()`). # Pull trial-level data from Redivis and save it to a data frame called "trials" trials <- get_trials(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current")  #> Measures adapted from the Rapid Online Assessment of Reading (Language Sounds, Sentence Reading, Word Reading) are covered under a Stanford Academic License (https://github.com/yeatmanlab/roar-mp/blob/main/LICENSE). #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table trials  # Now let's do some preliminary checks on the trials data trials |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of trials per dataset #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main  1323  trials |> count(task_id, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of trials per task #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>    task_id                      n #>    <chr>                    <int> #>  1 vocab                      242 #>  2 hearts-and-flowers         236 #>  3 trog                       153 #>  4 egma-math                  125 #>  5 swr                        120 #>  6 matrix-reasoning           108 #>  7 theory-of-mind             102 #>  8 same-different-selection    85 #>  9 mental-rotation             68 #> 10 memory-game                 52 #> 11 sre                         32 # Pull survey data from Redivis and save it to a data frame called "surveys" surveys <- get_surveys(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current")  #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table surveys  # Now let's do some preliminary checks on the trials data surveys |> count(dataset, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of surveys per dataset #> # A tibble: 1 × 2 #>   dataset                  n #>   <chr>                <int> #> 1 pilot_mpieva_de_main    63 # Pull up-to-date item parameters from Redivis item_parameters <- get_parameters(data_source = "levante-data-example:d0rt", version = "current") #> Fetching data for levante-data-example:d0rt #> --Fetching table parameters  item_parameters |> count(task_id, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of parameters per task #> # A tibble: 11 × 2 #>    task_id                      n #>    <chr>                    <int> #>  1 swr                       2776 #>  2 vocab                      912 #>  3 egma-math                  718 #>  4 theory-of-mind             398 #>  5 matrix-reasoning           302 #>  6 pa                         220 #>  7 trog                       194 #>  8 memory-game                 48 #>  9 mental-rotation             40 #> 10 same-different-selection    30 #> 11 hearts-and-flowers          20 # Pull data from any table within from Redivis – in this case, we're pulling the administrations table (each assignment is an administration) administrations <- get_raw_table(table_name = "administrations", data_source =  "levante-data-example-raw:bm7r")  #> Fetching data for levante-data-example-raw:bm7r #> --Fetching table administrations  administrations |> count(public_name, sort = TRUE) # returns the number of completions per assignment, based on that assignment's name #> # A tibble: 18 × 2 #>    public_name                     n #>    <chr>                       <int> #>  1 Paket 1                         2 #>  2 Paket 3                         2 #>  3 Group3_Roar                     1 #>  4 Group3_noR                      1 #>  5 Paket 2 Group 2                 1 #>  6 Paket 4 (kein Sprachtest)       1 #>  7 Paket 4 (mit Sprachtest)        1 #>  8 Paket 4 (mit Sprachtest) G2     1 #>  9 Paket2                          1 #> 10 Retest_G1&2_Roar                1 #> 11 Retest_G3                       1 #> 12 Retest_G3_R                     1 #> 13 Retest_Group1                   1 #> 14 Runde 1 (Nov 2025)              1 #> 15 Runde 2 (Januar 2026)           1 #> 16 Test_Group4_2                   1 #> 17 Test_Group4_TP1                 1 #> 18 Test_Survey_0825                1

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: R package accessing LEVANTE data. useful links: - overview LEVANTE project. - Details LEVANTE’s scoring psychometrics. - Details LEVANTE child tasks. - access LEVANTE data. Prior completing steps described link, access example dataset toy data. - Data browser. can browse scored trial level task public data using data browser. partner access additional datasets Redivis, also able load browse datasets data browser.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
code: # install.packages("devtools") # if you need the devtools package, uncomment this line devtools::install_git("levante-framework/levante-r")

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: collection get_() functions acquire LEVANTE data. example, use get_scores() download scored cognitive task data. understand scores produced — reproduce audit using LEVANTE model registry — see Scoring model registry vignette.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: Permission access data granted via Redivis, data-sharing platform used LEVANTE datasets. Individuals seeking access LEVANTE data must create account Redivis sign data use agreement. Datasets available public include levante-data-pilots levante-example-data. partner access data private repository, see page information set data access. access dataset Redivis, can use levante read data directly R. Please see levante user walkthrough information examples.

title: Download LEVANTE Data
text: addition name, dataset reference ID. required, specifying ID stable code specifying dataset names, dataset names can easily edited. find use dataset’s reference ID, follow steps: Go dataset’s page Redivis. example, link publicly available pilot data release https://stanford.redivis.com/datasets/68kn-csrddrz5x. , click “API Information” right navigation panel.  pop-, check box upper right hand corner “Qualified references.”  Record string used specify dataset, including name (“levante_data_pilots”“) reference ID (”68kn”). levante functions, string can used argument identify dataset (e.g., get_participants(data_source = “levante_data_pilots:68kn”)). can also pin specific dataset version appending reference, giving fully qualified name:hash:version string (e.g., get_scores(data_source = "levante_data_pilots:68kn:v1_0")). Pinning version makes analysis reproducible, whereas version = "current" (default) tracks latest release. call “get” function version = "current", levante reports qualified reference resolved , can copy pin future runs.

title: Get item parameters — get_parameters
